custom-yqWX4gyGv4MqEbhFZIUQZ.name: Deployment architecture

x-props.defaultValue:
Deployment architecture Link to EA tool

   · Cluster setup – Single or Multi-cluster or Stretched cluster set up
   · High Availability - Depict how High Availability (Active -Active or Active-Passive) is achieved within a Data Centre & across data centre
   · Disaster Recovery - Provide details of how DR is achieved with the details of the various components that require Automated or Manual  process for recovery.
   · Data Replication – Sync or Async
   · Shared Services – Depict the various shared middleware services (e.g., Kafka/Redis/AMQ) & Storage services S3/NFS/NAS that are used by application

custom-ll_hv4-RS8sDq5hT1jNTz.name: Potential failure scenarios

x-props.defaultValue:
Potential failure scenarios

   · Provide the details of current state and aggregable data loss as per business.
   · List down all the possible potential failure scenarios for the system under review from the perspective of system and service level.
   · List down all scenarios to address resiliency principle “Fault Isolation” for the product software.

custom-7CY88W3Lkt4KyNUVmRHyy.name: Resiliency & redundancy

x-props.defaultValue:
Resiliency & redundancy

   · List down the details of the complete tech stack, vendor products, open-source framework
   · Provide the compliance to the Technology Standards as provided by COE
   · Please provide decision / reasoning for choosing specific technology and associated implications
   · Please refer COE Confluence page for BHS (Buy Hold Sell) strategy for Tech Brick. View details here: Enterprise Technology Catalogue

custom-JvkTczU6dRHBpq_v4Nu0o.name: Testing plan

x-props.defaultValue:
Testing plan

   · PAT Applicability Criteria
   · 1. Applications that are part of DTDT=0/1/2 Journey, and
   · 2. Systems Available in PAT environment, and
   · 3. Projects/Changes that require unmasked production data for testing, and
   · 4. Deploying changes to existing servers (brown field)
   · 5. PAT Exemption: Testing for interfaces external to DBS can be exempted from PAT
